question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getNode()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes all nodes inactive (errors >= maxSubscribeAttempts) and their readmitTime in the future, so that tryReadmit(false) always returns inactive and tryReadmit(true) is only called for nodes that pass hasBlock(), but since getBlockRange() also times out, hasBlock() returns false for all, causing permanent BlockStreamException?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: drainGrpcBuffer()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that results in multiple concurrent calls to drainGrpcBuffer() for different grpcCall instances, each submitting a task to the single-thread executor, causing tasks to queue up and the executor's internal queue to grow without bound, eventually causing OOM?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBlockNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes blockNodeDiscoveryService.getBlockNodes() throw an unchecked exception, propagating through the synchronized getBlockNodes() and leaving nodes in an inconsistent state (old nodes closed but new nodes not set)?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doGet()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes node.streamBlocks() throw a BlockStreamException after partial block assembly, leaving the BlockAssembler's pending list in a non-empty state that is never cleared, causing subsequent block assembly to throw 'Received block items of a new block while the previous block is still pending'?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getNode()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes getBlockRange() return a range where lowerEndpoint() equals Long.MAX_VALUE, causing nextBlockNumber.set(Long.MAX_VALUE) when EARLIEST_AVAILABLE_BLOCK_NUMBER is used, and then shouldGetBlock() to return false, silently halting block ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getBlockNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes the synchronized getBlockNodes() block indefinitely (e.g., blockNodeDiscoveryService.getBlockNodes() hangs due to a database connection timeout), starving all threads that need to call doGet() and halting block ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: close()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes Objects.requireNonNull(nodes.get()) throw NullPointerException in close() if nodes was set to null by a concurrent operation, causing close() to fail and leaving the executor running indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: doGet()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes node.streamBlocks() throw an exception after onBlockStream() has already been called and the block has been partially persisted, causing the importer to retry the same block and potentially process it twice?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: drainGrpcBuffer()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes the executor's drain task run for an extended period (grpcCall.read() returning data slowly), blocking the single executor thread and preventing subsequent drain tasks from running, causing gRPC buffer memory to accumulate?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockNodeSubscriber.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getNode()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes all nodes fail tryReadmit(false) and all nodes fail hasBlock() in the inactive loop, causing BlockStreamException to be thrown on every polling cycle, generating a log storm that fills disk and causes secondary denial of service?
